_status: SIGNED-OFF 2026-07-07 — owner go-ahead ('push the rivers to prod', session claude/rivers-prod-deploy-env-es86lu). Danger anchors resolved 2026-07-06; Bourbeuse reclassified spring_fed_float->rain_flashy per Agnew (runoff-fed) at signoff. PRIOR STATUS: AWAITING SIGNOFF — calibrated in CFS from the authoritative NPS BUFF Float Guide tables (see buffalo-thresholds-captured.md), cross-validated against the live NPS dashboard for all 5 reference gauges. 24 anchors across 4 reaches, threshold_unit=cfs, all reference gauges are the polled USGS sites (poll param 00060 discharge). Supersedes the earlier Wild Bill's stage(ft) chart (Ponca ft-vs-flow discrepancy resolved: NPS categorizes by flow). Remaining [signoff]: (1) confirm the good/flowing split (best-effort lower-third good / upper-two-thirds flowing), (2) Pruitt gauge USGS number if a finer upper/middle split is wanted. Mechanical items status: park code 'buff' VERIFIED (nps.gov/buff, parkplanning.nps.gov/buff, NPS API parkCode=buff). NHD feature id and gauge coordinates/params/drainage areas remain UNKNOWN because USGS + NPS + National Map hosts are all denied by the current network policy (hard 403 on CONNECT) — NOT fabricated. Gauge coords/params/drainageArea should be auto-pulled from the USGS site service at ingest time (more reliable than hand transcription); NHD geometry via seed path or National Map query once egress allows (see scripts/config/nhd-rivers.json $buffalo_nhd_todo). Thresholds themselves are NPS-authoritative and dashboard-validated.
name: Buffalo National River
description: America's first National River (1972), a free-flowing, rain-driven Ozark river draining the Boston Mountains of northwest Arkansas. Flashy and seasonal in its upper reaches, near year-round floatable in its lower reaches. Managed by the National Park Service.
managingAuthority: National Park Service — Buffalo National River

regulations.text: Every vessel must carry a U.S. Coast Guard approved life vest (PFD) for each person aboard; children under age 13 must wear a securely fastened life vest while aboard any vessel.

Glass containers are prohibited within 100 feet of any river or stream in the park (and in caves, on trails or waterways), except in designated campgrounds, picnic sites, or vehicles on designated roads/parking areas.

Possession of polystyrene (Styrofoam) items — cups, plates, coolers, ice chests, containers — is prohibited while floating or camping, except in developed campgrounds, picnic areas, landings, roads and parking lots.
Vessels carrying food or beverages must have an attached closable mesh litter bag; food/beverages must be kept in a sealed cooler or spill-proof container.

accessLaw.value: UNKNOWN — Arkansas stream-access / navigability doctrine not yet captured

sections.name: Hailstone / Upper Wilderness (Boxley headwaters)
sections.description: Boxley-area headwaters creek run; rain-only, advanced, committing. Bone-dry much of the year.

representativeGauge.rationale: Boxley is the NPS float-guide reference gauge for the headwaters/Hailstone reach
representativeGauge.knownBias: headwaters gauge; confirm it tracks the runnable creek reach

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN — capture from NPS/USGS floatability guidance
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rain-driven flash rises; a committing wilderness run with no easy egress. Treat rising water as a serious hazard.

sections.name: Upper (Ponca to Pruitt)
sections.description: The classic upper Buffalo: Ponca to Kyle's Landing / Erbie / Ozark / Pruitt. Class I–II, flashy, spring-season float; Big Bluff and Hemmed-in Hollow scenery. The most level-sensitive reach.

putIn.name: Ponca (Steel Creek when too low to launch at Ponca)

representativeGauge.rationale: canonical upper-reach floatability gauge (Ponca) per outfitter/NPS usage

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: When too low to launch at Ponca, outfitters shift the put-in ~2 mi downriver to Steel Creek (8 mi to Kyle's). No numeric gauge cutoff is published — outfitters make a daily operational call. NUMERIC too_low anchor still UNKNOWN.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rain-driven flash rises; at high water the bluffs become hazardous (nowhere to eddy out). Strong current and debris. Treat rising water with high caution.

localKnowledge: Floatable season is typically March through June, occasionally into July with rainfall (Buffalo Outdoor Center).
The most level-sensitive reach on the river: too low and you drag, too high and the bluffs are hazardous.

sections.name: Middle (Pruitt to Gilbert / Tyler Bend)
sections.description: Longer float season, mellower than the upper. Carver, Hasty, Mt. Hersey, Woolum, Tyler Bend, Gilbert.

representativeGauge.rationale: St. Joe is the commonly cited middle-district gauge; Carver (07055780) covers the upper-middle transition
representativeGauge.knownBias: NPS chart keys the middle thresholds to 'Grinder's Ferry/Tyler Bend' — confirm that reference is USGS 07056000 (St. Joe) vs. a separate Tyler Bend gauge before ingest

sections.name: Lower (Gilbert to Buffalo City / White River confluence)
sections.description: Big pools, near year-round floatable, flatwater feel. Buffalo Point, Rush, Dillard's Ferry (Hwy 14).

representativeGauge.rationale: Harriet covers the upper-lower reach; Rush (07057000) covers the lowest reach before the confluence
representativeGauge.knownBias: NPS chart keys the lower thresholds to 'Dillard's Ferry/Buffalo Point' (Hwy 14) — confirm that reference is USGS 07056700 (Harriet) vs. a separate Buffalo Point gauge before ingest

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN — capture; low water mainly affects speed here, not runnability
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rises arrive from upstream rain; current and floating debris are the practical hazards on this calmer reach.

localKnowledge: Note: this reach reads more as flatwater than the flashy upper — a candidate for per-section river_type divergence if the uniform rain_flashy framing proves too conservative here.

accessPoints.name: Ponca
accessPoints.notes: primary upper put-in; used when level permits
accessPoints.description: NPS-managed river access at the Ponca low-water bridge on the upper Buffalo National River. Serves as the primary upper put-in for the Ponca-to-Pruitt district when water levels permit; boaters must portage the low-water bridge.
accessPoints.roadAccess: At the low-water bridge off AR Highways 74/43 near the community of Ponca; paved highway access.
accessPoints.facilities: Low-water bridge day-use river access; no campground or hookups at this access point.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap node 12544103523 (leisure=slipway, 'Ponca River Access Point'); corroborated by USGS gage 07055660 'Buffalo River at Ponca' https://waterdata.usgs.gov/monitoring-location/USGS-07055660/ (36.0225, -93.3547222, ~160m upstream)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: OSM slipway explicitly named 'Ponca River Access Point' at the Ponca low-water bridge; USGS gage ~160m away agrees. Within upper-Buffalo bounds.

accessPoints.name: Steel Creek
accessPoints.notes: low-water alternate put-in ~2 mi below Ponca
accessPoints.description: NPS-managed access and campground on the upper Buffalo National River about 2 miles below Ponca, commonly used as a low-water alternate put-in. The campground has walk-in tent sites and a separate equestrian area with seasonal flush restrooms and water.
accessPoints.roadAccess: Off AR Highway 74 about 3 miles east of Ponca; steep gravel entrance road descends to the campground and river.
accessPoints.facilities: Walk-in tent sites and equestrian sites; flush restrooms and water spigots (approx. mid-March to mid-November); no RV hookups.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap node (tourism=camp_site, 'Steel Creek Campground'); corroborated by Recreation.gov facility 10001451 https://www.recreation.gov/camping/campgrounds/10001451 (~36.0408, -93.3440)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Campground/access coordinate; two independent sources agree to ~150m. River put-in is at the campground's river frontage.

accessPoints.name: Kyle's Landing
accessPoints.description: NPS-managed access and tent campground on the upper Buffalo National River between Ponca and Jasper, a common take-out for the upper (Ponca/Steel Creek) float. Offers 33 tent-only sites (no RVs) with seasonal flush restrooms and water; reached by a steep, rough gravel road.
accessPoints.roadAccess: Off AR Highway 74; a steep, rough ~3-mile gravel access road, high-clearance/4WD vehicles recommended.
accessPoints.facilities: 33 tent-only sites (RVs/motorhomes prohibited); flush restrooms and water (approx. mid-March to mid-November); first-come, first-served.
accessPoints.coordinateSource: OpenStreetMap node 4173015846 (tourism=camp_site, operator=NPS, 'Kyle's Landing'); corroborated by Recreation.gov POI 258693 / campflare (36.0558, -93.2808) and allstays (36.05576, -93.28130)
accessPoints.coordinateNote: Three independent sources agree to under 50m. Lies just east of the -93.30 guide bound, which is consistent with river mile 16.7 (downstream/east of Ponca and Steel Creek).
